relationship_to_target_domain.required_comparison: Report every synthetic result by locale, TTS engine, and voice, and keep the professional-podcast control separate.
relationship_to_target_domain.reason: Multi-voice synthetic speech is controlled, reproducible evidence for a pragmatic recommendation on clean produced speech. It can inform model selection without claiming universal real-world superiority or release-quality WER.

tts_candidates.model: installed de-DE system voice selected and captured per run
tts_candidates.integration: narrow Swift-to-C ABI called from Rust
tts_candidates.license: Apple platform API and installed system voice terms
tts_candidates.decision_after_pilot: Retain as the deterministic platform baseline even if an open model wins product selection.

tts_candidates.integration: pinned Python reference runner; direct repository-owned MLX boundary evaluated after measurements
tts_candidates.license: MIT runtime; Apache-2.0 model artifact; every file is byte-counted and SHA-256 pinned in spikes/qwen3-tts-mlx-reference/model-manifest.json
tts_candidates.decision_after_pilot: Compare accepting mlx-audio with owning only the required Qwen3-TTS path directly over official MLX.

tts_candidates.integration: pinned Python reference runner first; direct repository-owned MLX boundary only after calibration
tts_candidates.license: Apache-2.0 converted model and MIT reference runtime; all 14 snapshot files are byte-counted and SHA-256 pinned in spikes/tts-calibration/qwen3-tts-1.7b-voicedesign-bf16.json
tts_candidates.decision_after_pilot: Advance two description-pinned voices per locale only if German and English content fidelity and operational cost justify the 1.7B variant.

tts_candidates.integration: pinned Python reference runner with a native German preset; production use requires a separate commercial license or provider API
tts_candidates.license: CC-BY-NC-4.0 model and supplied voice references; MIT reference runtime; reference evaluation only under the repository dependency policy
tts_candidates.decision_after_pilot: If native German and other European-language quality materially lead, evaluate the hosted Mistral API or a commercial weight license; do not ship the CC-BY-NC artifact.

tts_candidates.integration: pinned Python reference runner with two native voices per locale; reference-only under current model license
tts_candidates.license: CC-BY-NC-4.0 model and supplied voice references; MIT reference runtime; all 27 snapshot files are byte-counted and SHA-256 pinned in spikes/tts-calibration/voxtral-tts-4b-mlx-bf16.json
tts_candidates.decision_after_pilot: Use BF16 to separate quantization effects from model-family quality; production still requires hosted use or another commercial license.

tts_candidates.integration: one-passage German calibration before any full-matrix promotion
tts_candidates.license: MIT model and runtime; all 14 snapshot files are pinned, while absent advertised voice files and the inactive AudioSeal path are explicit capability limits in spikes/tts-calibration/kugelaudio-0-open-bf16.json
tts_candidates.decision_after_pilot: Advance only if the German-focused quality materially adds value despite the roughly 9B-parameter and 19 GB inference footprint.

tts_candidates.runtime: Resemble upstream PyTorch/MPS reference; current MLX conversion requires separate verification
tts_candidates.integration: deferred until voice-prompt rights, digests, and current Apple-Silicon runtime are accepted
tts_candidates.license: MIT code/model family; every reference voice and converted artifact requires separate rights and provenance review
tts_candidates.decision_after_pilot: Advance only if intelligibility, prosody, or operational evidence adds value beyond Qwen3-TTS and the system baseline.

tts_candidates.integration: provider API reference runner with credentials supplied only through the environment
tts_candidates.source_revision: hosted-service-observed-2026-07-20; immutable provider snapshot not exposed
tts_candidates.license: Alibaba Cloud service terms; no local model weights
tts_candidates.decision_after_pilot: Use as an English provider-voice quality ceiling; do not infer German or local-model quality from its score.

external_evidence.scope: Provider-selected voices in the provider-voice arena; this is not German-language or local-runtime evidence.

candidate_review.promotion_rule: Pin every artifact first, run the bounded German/English calibration, reject unstable or unjustifiably expensive candidates, then freeze the surviving full matrix before materializing all passages.

execution_policy.matrix: Calibrate one passage per locale before the full run. The surviving matrix targets two voices each from Apple, Qwen3 VoiceDesign, and Voxtral BF16; every generated artifact is transcribed by the five required ASR backends using identical digest-checked normalized PCM.
execution_policy.voice_selection: Prefer distinct native-locale voices and different presentation profiles when metadata permits; record stable provider or model voice IDs and do not infer demographic traits from voice names.

repository_audio.scope: Redistributable clean-speech fixtures with an accepted rights review, complete attribution, and a passed lossless-versus-codec ASR control.

metrics.diagnosis: Attribute a roundtrip error to TTS, ASR, or unresolved only after listening to the generated audio and comparing the same audio across ASR backends.

exit_criteria: All source text and model artifacts are revision- and digest-pinned before measured execution.
The Apple system baseline completes a Rust-owned synthesis lifecycle including cancellation and generated-audio ownership.
Qwen3-TTS completes the same German passage set through the pinned mlx-audio reference runner.
The bounded engine calibration freezes exact Qwen VoiceDesign, Voxtral BF16, and any promoted challenger artifacts before the full multi-voice run.
Each measured locale covers at least three pinned voices across all three required TTS engines, with results reported per voice.
All generated PCM is passed unchanged to all five required ASR backends.
Results separate lexical content errors from punctuation and capitalization differences.
At least one blinded pronunciation and prosody review is recorded per candidate.
The evidence supports a written dependency decision: retain mlx-audio, build a narrow direct official-MLX adapter, or stop the candidate.
